title: Dead Beat Source Trail
description: A source-trail enrichment for Dead Beat around software rendering, live wall text, and bell-synthesis lineage.
blurb: a source-trail enrichment for Dead Beat with public context.
one_sentence_claim: Dead Beat is a browser-playable software-rendered room whose public authority trail should connect the live page, later software-rendering experiments, and the chewgum-dsp bell-synthesis extraction.
why_this_matters: Dead Beat is already public, but its page has only a compact source trail. This packet tests whether the adapter can stage a truthful enrichment without creating a new public artifact.
principle: existing public artifact enrichment, not new public publication
validation_idea: Confirm that all cited surfaces are public and that the target path already exists, so the correct human decision is enrichment rather than a new page.

source_trail.text: Dead Beat public page: browser-playable software-rendered room written in Rust and WebAssembly, with live-editable wall text.
Phosphor public page: later raycaster experiment that keeps the software-rendering lane visible as a public trail.
Falling Hall public page: later software-rendered descent that explicitly cites Dead Beat's bell-synthesis lineage.
Triangle Engines public toy: isolates native triangle versus NES-style PeriodicWave behavior used in the chewgum-dsp lineage.
chewgum-dsp v0.1.0 public repository: extracted Web Audio helpers for NES-style triangle wave and bell synthesis.

claims_requiring_signoff: Any claim that Dead Beat is the origin point for a reusable audio-library lineage should be human-reviewed against the public source trail.
